AC-001-c.command: Get-ChildItem src-tauri/src/audio_toolkit/vad -Recurse -Filter *.rs | % { [pscustomobject]@{ Path=$_.FullName; Lines=(Get-Content $_ | Measure-Object -Line).Lines } } | Sort-Object Lines -Descending
steps: Run the command above from repo root
Confirm every listed file has Lines <= 800
Record max line count in journal.md

AC-002-b.command: Manual timed run on silence-heavy fixture
steps: Pick a silence-heavy fixture under eval/fixtures/ (>= 30% silence); record its path
Set vad_prefilter_enabled = false in settings; transcribe the fixture; record wall-time in journal.md as BASELINE_MS
Set vad_prefilter_enabled = true; transcribe the same fixture; record wall-time as PREFILTER_MS
Confirm PREFILTER_MS < BASELINE_MS and record the percentage delta in journal.md and in the PR body
Pick or curate a music/silence-mixed fixture under eval/fixtures/; record its path

With vad_prefilter_enabled = false, transcribe and dump word list with timestamps; count words whose [start_us, end_us] lies entirely inside a hand-labeled non-speech region; record as BASELINE_HALLUC
With vad_prefilter_enabled = true, repeat the count; record as PREFILTER_HALLUC
Confirm PREFILTER_HALLUC <= BASELINE_HALLUC; record both numbers in journal.md and in the PR body

AC-003-b.command: Invoke the waveform-diff agent on the standard splice fixture with vad_refine_boundaries=true; confirm preview vs. export seam parity within 1 sample across all cuts; attach JSON to journal.md

AC-003-c.command: Invoke the cut-drift-fuzzer agent for 1000 seeded ops with vad_refine_boundaries=true; confirm zero cumulative duration drift, monotonic time maps, zero panics

AC-003-d.command: SHA-256 comparison of fixture export with vad_refine_boundaries=false vs. pre-feature baseline
steps: Before the feature lands, capture SHA-256 of the standard splice fixture export; record in journal.md as BASELINE_SHA
After the feature lands with vad_refine_boundaries=false, re-export the same fixture via the same command
Compute SHA-256 of the new export; confirm it equals BASELINE_SHA
Record both SHAs in journal.md
Run scripts/setup-env.ps1 first
Remove any existing Silero model file from the model cache dir
Launch the app via the monitored script
Open Transcription settings; enable 'Pre-filter silences before transcribing'; click Download
Confirm the Silero ONNX appears on disk and its SHA-256 matches the catalog entry
Toggle the setting off and on; close the app; relaunch; confirm the setting persists
Record findings + timestamps in journal.md
From repo root run npm run build and confirm exit 0
Open src/bindings.ts and grep for vad_prefilter_enabled and vad_refine_boundaries
Confirm both fields are present and are typed as boolean
Confirm bindings.ts was regenerated (not hand-edited) by checking git blame on the new lines
Run scripts/setup-env.ps1 first
Launch the app via the monitored script
Open Transcription settings; toggle 'Pre-filter silences before transcribing' off then on; confirm the change persists across restart
Open Editor settings; toggle 'Use VAD to refine splice boundaries' off then on; confirm persistence
Record each toggle with a timestamp and operator initials in journal.md

AC-007-a.command: Invoke .github/skills/dep-hygiene; run cargo machete in src-tauri; confirm zero unused dependencies

AC-007-c.command: Confirm .github/skills/handy-legacy-pruning/SKILL.md gained a heading containing 'VAD reintroduced' and that its body references R-002, R-003, R-004 verbatim; the referenced BLUEPRINT sections above describe the module layout the skill doc must match

steps: Open the PR description
Confirm it contains BASELINE_MS / PREFILTER_MS (from AC-002-b) with the percentage delta
Confirm it contains BASELINE_HALLUC / PREFILTER_HALLUC (from AC-002-c)
Confirm it contains BASELINE_SHA (from AC-003-d) proving byte-identical default behavior
Block merge if any number is missing or reads 'should be better'
